negative_cases.why_wrong: Indirect Branch Null Balance: forces the main output to zero, so coarse compilation passes but behavioral stimulus points fail.
Indirect Branch Null Balance: uses a shifted or inverted decision condition that can pass one mode but fails boundary cases.
Indirect Branch Null Balance: mishandles reset or initialization, passing steady-state samples but failing startup or recovery checks.
Indirect Branch Null Balance: keeps the main datapath plausible while reporting the wrong metric value.
Indirect Branch Null Balance: uses a wrong gain or scale factor, leaving shape plausible while calibrated checks fail.
